response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer with strong breadth across web, mobile, and embedded domains, but the role specifically requires a hands-on C++ developer with 8-12 years of C++, STL, multi-threading, message brokers, MQTT/REST IoT work, and IEC 62443 secure development experience. The resume shows only a brief early-career C/C++ stint at National Instruments (Dec 2007 - Jun 2008) and a C++ bachelor's thesis project; the bulk of the last ~15 years has been TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no evidence of industrial automation, IEC 62443 SDL, OPC UA/Modbus/DNP3 protocols, or recent C++ architecture work, making this a poor fit despite strong general engineering credentials.
strengths: Long tenure and seniority with architectural and full-stack delivery experience
Some early C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and on a wave-field-synthesis thesis
Strong testing/TDD discipline and mentoring/workshop track record
concerns: No substantial or recent C++ / STL production experience (last C++ role ended ~2008)
No demonstrated IEC 62443, SDL, or industrial cybersecurity background
No evidence of industrial-automation protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850/104, DNP3) or MQTT/message-broker IoT systems

key_factors.factor: Missing industrial automation and IEC 62443 background
Strong general senior engineering and architecture experience
